summary: Leveraged buyout debt on software companies done 2 years ago is at serious risk
summary_extended: Baker warns that leveraged buyout debt on software companies executed roughly two years prior (circa 2021-2022) is at serious risk of stress or default. These deals were done at high valuation multiples and are now carrying 3-4x leverage in high-yield debt structures, making them highly sensitive to revenue deterioration. Baker's core concern is that AI disruption is undermining the revenue models of the legacy SaaS and software companies that were taken private in those buyouts, eroding the cash flows needed to service that debt. He expresses strong conviction, saying he would be 'very worried' holding such securities, framing this as a direct credit risk warning rather than a speculative concern. The investment implication is to avoid or short the high-yield debt tranches of PE-backed software LBOs from that vintage, though no specific publicly traded securities are named.
detail: Baker warns that private equity software buyouts done 2 years ago at high multiples, now levered 3-4x with high-yield debt, face severe stress as AI disrupts their revenue models. He says he would be 'very worried' holding such securities.
confidence_evidence: Baker uses strong language ('very worried') and frames it as a direct risk warning, not speculation
quote_fragment: very worried if I were holding 3 or 4 times levered high yield security for a buyout done two years ago
